agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics platform expanding from single-region EU to EU + US + APAC with strict data residency (GDPR/CCPA), region-bound courier assignment, a global operations dashboard, graceful degradation, and sub-100ms regional latency. The architecture uses a region-primary ownership model: each region owns its customer and courier data authoritatively, enforces residency at the infrastructure boundary, and publishes anonymized/aggregated operational metrics to a global read-only plane for the dashboard. Cross-region coordination is event-driven and asynchronous, with no synchronous cross-region calls on the customer hot path.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Regional Customer Service","responsibility":"Owns customer identity, PII, order history, and preferences for customers domiciled in that region. Enforces data residency — no replication of PII outside the region.","boundaries":"Deployed per-region. Database is region-local. No cross-region read/write. Exposes APIs only within its region's network boundary."},{"name":"Regional Courier Service","responsibility":"Manages courier profiles, availability, location tracking, and assignment eligibility. A courier is registered to exactly one region and can only be assigned deliveries within that region.","boundaries":"Deployed per-region. Courier records never replicate cross-region. Assignment logic enforces region constraint as an invariant."},{"name":"Regional Delivery Orchestrator","responsibility":"Coordinates order intake, courier assignment, route optimization, and delivery lifecycle within a single region. This is the core workflow engine.","boundaries":"Per-region deployment. Calls Regional Customer Service and Regional Courier Service within the same region only. Publishes domain events (delivery.created, delivery.completed, delivery.failed) to the regional event bus."},{"name":"Regional Event Bus","responsibility":"Intra-region event backbone for domain events. Fan-out to regional consumers and to the Global Metrics Aggregator via a sanitized bridge.","boundaries":"Per-region. Events containing PII stay on this bus. Only the Metrics Bridge component reads from it and forwards anonymized/aggregated data."},{"name":"Metrics Bridge (per-region)","responsibility":"Reads domain events from the regional event bus, strips all PII, computes regional aggregates (delivery counts, SLA metrics, courier utilization), and publishes sanitized metric events to the Global Metrics Topic.","boundaries":"Runs within the region. Strictly one-way: region → global. Never sends PII. Acts as the Anti-Corruption Layer between regional data and the global plane."},{"name":"Global Metrics Aggregator","responsibility":"Consumes sanitized metric events from all regions. Materializes a unified read model for the Global Operations Dashboard. Stores only non-PII aggregate data.","boundaries":"Deployed in a single coordination region (or multi-region active-active for HA). Contains zero PII. Read-only from the perspective of regional services."},{"name":"Global Operations Dashboard","responsibility":"Real-time dashboard showing cross-region KPIs: deliveries in progress, SLA compliance, courier utilization, regional health status.","boundaries":"Reads exclusively from the Global Metrics Aggregator. No direct access to any regional database or service."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Entry point for all customer and courier traffic in a region. Enforces authentication, rate limiting, and routes requests to regional services. GeoDNS directs users to their home region's gateway.","boundaries":"Per-region. Terminates TLS, enforces that requests are served by the correct region for the requesting user's domicile."},{"name":"Regional Resilience Layer","responsibility":"Circuit breakers, local caches, and fallback logic to provide degraded service when upstream dependencies (e.g., a regional DB replica) become unavailable. Includes a static/cached read path for critical customer-facing pages.","boundaries":"Per-region. Activates during partial failures to serve cached order status, estimated delivery times, and basic courier tracking."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Regional Delivery Orchestrator","contract":"REST/gRPC — CreateDelivery, GetDeliveryStatus, CancelDelivery. All requests include region-scoped customer ID.","coupling_type":"synchronous, region-local"},{"from":"Regional Delivery Orchestrator","to":"Regional Customer Service","contract":"gRPC — GetCustomer, ValidateCustomerRegion. Region-local only.","coupling_type":"synchronous, region-local"},{"from":"Regional Delivery Orchestrator","to":"Regional Courier Service","contract":"gRPC — FindAvailableCouriers, AssignCourier, ReleaseCourier. Region-scoped queries only.","coupling_type":"synchronous, region-local"},{"from":"Regional Delivery Orchestrator","to":"Regional Event Bus","contract":"Async publish — DeliveryCreated, DeliveryAssigned, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed events.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, fire-and-forget"},{"from":"Regional Event Bus","to":"Metrics Bridge","contract":"Async subscribe — consumes all delivery and courier domain events within the region.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, region-local consumer"},{"from":"Metrics Bridge","to":"Global Metrics Topic","contract":"Async publish — sanitized metric events (no PII): RegionDeliveryMetric, RegionCourierUtilization, RegionSLASnapshot.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, cross-region, one-way"},{"from":"Global Metrics Aggregator","to":"Global Metrics Topic","contract":"Async subscribe — consumes sanitized metrics from all regions.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, multi-region consumer"},{"from":"Global Operations Dashboard","to":"Global Metrics Aggregator","contract":"WebSocket/SSE for real-time push of aggregated metrics; REST for historical queries.","coupling_type":"synchronous read, no PII"}],"data_flow":"1) GeoDNS routes customer to their home Regional API Gateway based on domicile, not physical location. 2) Gateway forwards to Regional Delivery Orchestrator which coordinates with Regional Customer Service and Regional Courier Service — all within region. 3) Orchestrator publishes domain events to Regional Event Bus. 4) Metrics Bridge consumes events, strips PII, computes aggregates, publishes to Global Metrics Topic. 5) Global Metrics Aggregator materializes a unified view. 6) Dashboard reads from Aggregator via WebSocket. PII never crosses a regional boundary at any point in this flow.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Data Sovereignty by Design (Region-Primary Ownership)","rationale":"Each region is the single authoritative owner of its customer and courier data. No cross-region replication of PII. This satisfies GDPR data residency and CCPA compliance structurally — not by policy, but by architecture. There is no path for PII to leave the region."},{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (Metrics Bridge)","rationale":"The Metrics Bridge acts as an ACL between the rich, PII-laden regional domain model and the global metrics plane. It translates domain events into anonymized aggregates, preventing model leakage and ensuring compliance."},{"pattern":"CQRS (Command-Query Responsibility Segregation)","rationale":"Regional services handle commands (create delivery, assign courier). The Global Metrics Aggregator is a purely read-side projection optimized for dashboard queries. This separation allows independent scaling and avoids the global plane needing write access to regional data."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture with Async Bridging","rationale":"Cross-region communication is strictly asynchronous and one-way (region → global). No synchronous cross-region calls exist on the customer hot path, eliminating cross-region latency from the P99 budget and removing cross-region availability coupling."},{"pattern":"Cell-Based Architecture","rationale":"Each region is an independent cell with its own complete stack (gateway, services, database, event bus). Cells are isolated — a failure in the EU cell does not propagate to US or APAC. This directly supports the degraded-service-on-failure requirement."},{"pattern":"Bulkhead Isolation","rationale":"Regional isolation acts as a bulkhead. Additionally, within each region, the Resilience Layer provides circuit breakers and cached fallbacks to deliver degraded service during partial outages."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Delivery Management (per-region)","classification":"core","aggregates":["Delivery (root: DeliveryId, contains pickup/dropoff locations, status, assigned courier, customer reference — all region-scoped)","DeliveryAssignment (value object capturing courier-to-delivery binding with region constraint invariant)"]},{"name":"Customer Management (per-region)","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Customer (root: CustomerId, contains PII, domicile region — region is immutable after creation, enforces residency)"]},{"name":"Courier Management (per-region)","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Courier (root: CourierId, contains profile, home region, availability status, current location — region is immutable, enforces assignment constraint)"]},{"name":"Global Operations Intelligence","classification":"generic","aggregates":["RegionalMetricsSnapshot (root: RegionId + TimeBucket, contains delivery counts, SLA percentages, courier utilization — no PII)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Delivery Management","downstream":"Global Operations Intelligence","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer — Metrics Bridge strips PII from delivery events and publishes anonymized aggregates. Global context conforms to a sanitized published language."},{"upstream":"Customer Management","downstream":"Delivery Management","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Delivery Management queries Customer Management for validation but does not own customer data. Customer context dictates the model."},{"upstream":"Courier Management","downstream":"Delivery Management","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Delivery Management queries Courier Management for availability and assignment. Courier context owns courier identity and region binding."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"No cross-region data replication for PII","gains":"Structural GDPR/CCPA compliance — impossible to violate residency by misconfiguration. Simpler audit posture. No cross-region replication lag for customer data.","sacrifices":"A customer who relocates from EU to US requires a formal data migration process (not a simple DB sync). Global customer search is not possible without a separate anonymized index.","alternatives_considered":["Encrypted cross-region replication with key management per region — rejected because it adds compliance surface area and key management complexity while not being structurally compliant","Single global database with row-level region tagging — rejected because a single misconfigured query or replication change could violate residency"]},{"decision":"Asynchronous-only cross-region communication","gains":"Eliminates cross-region latency from customer-facing paths (supports <100ms). Eliminates cross-region availability coupling (EU outage doesn't affect US). Simple failure model.","sacrifices":"Global dashboard shows near-real-time data (seconds of delay, not true real-time). Global consistency is eventual. Cannot perform synchronous cross-region transactions.","alternatives_considered":["Synchronous cross-region API calls for dashboard — rejected because it adds 100-300ms latency, creates availability coupling, and is unnecessary for a metrics dashboard","Global distributed database (e.g., CockroachDB/Spanner) — rejected because it introduces cross-region write latency and creates a path for PII to replicate"]},{"decision":"Cell-based architecture with independent stacks per region","gains":"Strong blast radius containment. Independent deployment and scaling per region. Regulatory independence — each region can have its own compliance configuration.","sacrifices":"Operational overhead of managing three independent deployments. Code duplication across regions (mitigated by shared IaC and CI/CD). Higher infrastructure cost than a single global deployment.","alternatives_considered":["Single global deployment with region routing at the application layer — rejected because it creates a single blast radius and makes residency enforcement application-level rather than infrastructure-level"]},{"decision":"Metrics Bridge as explicit component rather than direct event forwarding","gains":"Clear compliance boundary. Testable independently. Can be audited to prove no PII crosses regions. Allows metric schema evolution independent of domain events.","sacrifices":"Additional component to build, deploy, and monitor per region. Slight increase in event processing latency for metrics.","alternatives_considered":["Direct event forwarding with PII field filtering — rejected because implicit filtering is fragile; a new PII field added to a domain event could leak if the filter isn't updated"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., LATAM, MEA)","evolution_path":"The cell-based architecture means adding a region is deploying a new cell using the same IaC templates. The Metrics Bridge pattern means the Global Aggregator automatically picks up events from the new region's topic. No changes to existing regions required."},{"area":"Region-specific compliance rules","evolution_path":"Each region's services can have region-specific configuration (data retention periods, consent models, audit logging levels) without affecting other regions. The cell boundary is the compliance boundary."},{"area":"Dashboard evolution","evolution_path":"The Global Metrics Aggregator is a separate bounded context with its own read model. New dashboard views, alerting rules, or ML-based anomaly detection can be added without touching regional services."},{"area":"Cross-region customer migration","evolution_path":"A dedicated migration workflow can be built that exports customer data from the source region, transmits it securely, and imports it into the destination region with proper consent. This is an explicit, auditable process rather than implicit replication."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Customer domicile region binding","reason":"A customer's home region is immutable in normal operation. This is intentional — it enforces data residency structurally. Changing it requires an explicit migration workflow, not a simple update."},{"area":"Courier region binding","reason":"A courier's operating region is immutable. This enforces the business rule that a EU courier cannot be assigned to a US delivery. Cross-region courier operations would require a new business process, not a configuration change."},{"area":"No synchronous cross-region calls on the hot path","reason":"This is a load-bearing architectural constraint. Adding a synchronous cross-region call would violate the <100ms latency requirement and the regional isolation model. Any future feature requiring cross-region coordination must use async patterns."},{"area":"PII boundary at the Metrics Bridge","reason":"The Metrics Bridge is the sole gate between regional PII and the global plane. This boundary must never be bypassed. New metric requirements must go through the Bridge, not around it."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Clock skew and event ordering across regions causes inconsistent global dashboard metrics","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use logical timestamps (vector clocks or Lamport timestamps) on metric events. The Global Aggregator uses time-bucketed aggregation windows (e.g., 10-second tumbling windows) that tolerate ordering variance. Dashboard shows time-bucket granularity, not event-level precision."},{"risk":"Metrics Bridge accidentally forwards PII due to domain event schema evolution","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Metrics Bridge uses an explicit allowlist of fields to forward (not a denylist). Automated CI tests verify that the Bridge output schema contains zero PII-classified fields. Schema registry enforces that new domain event fields are tagged with a PII classification. Audit log on all Bridge output."},{"risk":"GeoDNS misroutes a customer to the wrong region, causing latency or compliance issues","severity":"high","mitigation":"GeoDNS is the first-hop routing only. The Regional API Gateway performs a secondary check: it validates the customer's domicile region (from the auth token) against the current region. If mismatched, it returns a redirect to the correct region's gateway. This is a defense-in-depth measure."},{"risk":"EU region failure causes total loss of service for EU customers instead of graceful degradation","severity":"high","mitigation":"Within the EU cell, deploy across multiple availability zones. The Resilience Layer maintains a read-through cache of recent order statuses, courier locations, and delivery ETAs. During a partial outage (e.g., database failover), customers see cached data with a staleness indicator. New orders queue locally and process when the database recovers. Full-region failure (all AZs) is mitigated by multi-AZ redundancy — a simultaneous loss of all AZs is an accepted residual risk."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of managing three independent cells leads to configuration drift","severity":"medium","mitigation":"All three regions are deployed from the same Infrastructure-as-Code templates with region-specific parameter files. CI/CD pipeline deploys to all regions with canary rollouts. Configuration drift detection runs daily and alerts on divergence."},{"risk":"Global Metrics Aggregator becomes a single point of failure for the dashboard","severity":"low","mitigation":"The Aggregator is a read-only projection that can be rebuilt from the Global Metrics Topic. Deploy it in a multi-AZ configuration in the coordination region. If it fails, the dashboard shows stale data; no regional operations are affected."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout in three stages. Phase 1: Refactor the existing EU monolith into the cell architecture (Regional Gateway, Delivery Orchestrator, Customer Service, Courier Service, Event Bus) while remaining single-region. Validate the architecture works. Phase 2: Deploy the US cell using the same IaC templates, implement the Metrics Bridge and Global Aggregator, bring up the Global Dashboard. Phase 3: Deploy the APAC cell. Each phase is independently valuable and reduces risk. Start with the Metrics Bridge early — it is the highest-compliance-risk component and needs the most testing.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/modules/regional-cell/ — IaC module defining a complete regional cell (gateway, services, DB, event bus, metrics bridge)","infra/environments/{eu,us,apac}.tfvars — region-specific parameters (cloud region, compliance config, retention policies)","services/delivery-orchestrator/ — core workflow service, region-scoped","services/customer-service/ — customer PII management, region-scoped","services/courier-service/ — courier management with region-binding invariant","services/metrics-bridge/ — PII-stripping ACL component, per-region","services/global-metrics-aggregator/ — cross-region read model for dashboard","services/global-dashboard/ — real-time operations UI consuming aggregator","libs/domain-events/schemas/ — shared event schema definitions with PII field classification","libs/compliance/residency-validator/ — reusable module for domicile-region validation at gateway and service layers","docs/adr/001-multi-region-cell-architecture.md","docs/adr/002-no-cross-region-pii-replication.md","docs/adr/003-async-only-cross-region-communication.md","docs/runbooks/region-failover.md","docs/runbooks/customer-region-migration.md"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture for regional isolation. CQRS for separating regional commands from global reads. Anti-Corruption Layer (Metrics Bridge) for compliance boundary. Event-driven async for cross-region communication. Defense-in-depth for residency enforcement (GeoDNS + Gateway validation + service-level checks). Infrastructure-as-Code with parameterized regional deployments. Schema registry with PII classification for domain events.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["DO NOT create any database replication across regions — this is the most common way to accidentally violate data residency","DO NOT add synchronous cross-region calls to any customer-facing path — this will blow the 100ms latency budget and create availability coupling","DO NOT use a denylist approach for PII filtering in the Metrics Bridge — use an explicit allowlist of non-PII fields","DO NOT route customers by physical IP geolocation alone — use domicile region from the customer profile/auth token as the authoritative routing signal","DO NOT share a single event bus across regions — each region must have its own bus with the Metrics Bridge as the sole cross-region conduit","DO NOT allow courier region to be mutable without an explicit business process — a simple UPDATE would silently break the assignment constraint","DO NOT deploy a global distributed database (Spanner/CockroachDB) as the primary store — it creates implicit cross-region replication paths that undermine residency guarantees"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Data residency enforcement through cell isolation — this is a well-established pattern","Async-only cross-region communication for latency and isolation","Metrics Bridge as Anti-Corruption Layer for compliance","Cell-based architecture for blast radius containment","CQRS for separating regional writes from global reads"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact latency budget breakdown within the 100ms target depends on cloud provider region placement and database choice — needs benchmarking","Degraded service UX during regional partial outage needs product input on which features to prioritize in degraded mode","Global dashboard real-time expectations need quantification — 'real-time' could mean 1s or 30s, which affects Metrics Bridge batching strategy"],"assumptions":["Each customer has a single domicile region that determines data residency","Couriers operate in exactly one region and do not need cross-region mobility","The Global Operations Dashboard is used by internal operations staff, not customers","Current EU deployment can be refactored into the cell architecture without a full rewrite","Cloud provider supports the three target regions with equivalent service availability"]}}
agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics platform expanding from single-region EU to EU + US + APAC with strict data residency (GDPR/CCPA), region-bound courier assignment, a global operations dashboard, graceful degradation, and sub-100ms regional latency. The architecture uses a region-primary ownership model: each region owns its customer and courier data authoritatively, enforces residency at the infrastructure boundary, and publishes anonymized/aggregated operational metrics to a global

The Metrics Bridge pattern means the Global Aggregator automatically picks up events from the new region's topic. No changes to existing regions required."},{"area":"Region-specific compliance rules","evolution_path":"Each region's services can have region-specific configuration (data retention periods, consent models, audit logging levels) without affecting other regions. The cell boundary is the compliance boundary."},{"area":"Dashboard evolution","evolution_path":"The Global Metrics Aggregator is a separate bounded context with its own read model. New dashboard views, alerting rules, or ML-based anomaly detection can be added without touching regional services."},{"area":"Cross-region customer migration","evolution_path":"A dedicated migration workflow can be built that exports customer data from the source region, transmits it securely, and imports it into the destination region with proper consent. This is an explicit, auditable process rather than implicit replication."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Customer domicile region binding","reason":"A customer's home region is immutable in normal operation. This is intentional — it enforces data residency structurally. Changing it requires an explicit migration workflow, not a simple update."},{"area":"Courier region binding","reason":"A courier's operating region is immutable. This enforces the business rule that a EU courier cannot be assigned to a US delivery. Cross-region courier operations would require a new business process, not a configuration change."},{"area":"No synchronous cross-region calls on the hot path","reason":"This is a load-bearing architectural constraint. Adding a synchronous cross-region call would violate the <100ms latency requirement and the regional isolation model. Any future feature requiring cross-region coordination must use async patterns."},{"area":"PII boundary at the Metrics Bridge","reason":"The Metrics Bridge is the sole gate between regional PII and the global plane. This boundary must never be bypassed. New metric requirements must go through the Bridge, not around it."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Clock skew and event ordering across regions causes inconsistent global dashboard metrics","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use logical timestamps (vector clocks or Lamport timestamps) on metric events. The Global Aggregator uses time-bucketed aggregation windows (e.g., 10-second tumbling windows) that tolerate ordering variance. Dashboard shows time-bucket granularity, not event-level precision."},{"risk":"Metrics Bridge accidentally forwards PII due to domain event schema evolution","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Metrics Bridge uses an explicit allowlist of fields to forward (not a denylist). Automated CI tests verify that the Bridge output schema contains zero PII-classified fields. Schema registry enforces that new domain event fields are tagged with a PII classification. Audit log on all Bridge output."},{"risk":"GeoDNS misroutes a customer to the wrong region, causing latency or compliance issues","severity":"high","mitigation":"GeoDNS is the first-hop routing only. The Regional API Gateway performs a secondary check: it validates the customer's domicile region (from the auth token) against the current region. If mismatched, it returns a redirect to the correct region's gateway. This is a defense-in-depth measure."},{"risk":"EU region failure causes total loss of service for EU customers instead of graceful degradation","severity":"high","mitigation":"Within the EU cell, deploy across multiple availability zones. The Resilience Layer maintains a read-through cache of recent order statuses, courier locations, and delivery ETAs. During a partial outage (e.g., database failover), customers see cached data with a staleness indicator. New orders queue locally and process when the database recovers. Full-region failure (all AZs) is mitigated by multi-AZ redundancy — a simultaneous loss of all AZs is an accepted residual risk."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of managing three independent cells leads to configuration drift","severity":"medium","mitigation":"All three regions are deployed from the same Infrastructure-as-Code templates with region-specific parameter files. CI/CD pipeline deploys to all regions with canary rollouts. Configuration drift detection runs daily and alerts on divergence."},{"risk":"Global Metrics Aggregator becomes a single point of failure for the dashboard","severity":"low","mitigation":"The Aggregator is a read-only projection that can be rebuilt from the Global Metrics Topic. Deploy it in a multi-AZ configuration in the coordination region. If it fails, the dashboard shows stale data; no regional operations are affected."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout in three stages. Phase 1: Refactor the existing EU monolith into the cell architecture (Regional Gateway, Delivery Orchestrator, Customer Service, Courier Service, Event Bus) while remaining single-region. Validate the architecture works. Phase 2: Deploy the US cell using the same IaC templates, implement the Metrics Bridge and Global Aggregator, bring up the Global Dashboard. Phase 3: Deploy the APAC cell. Each phase is independently valuable and reduces risk. Start with the Metrics Bridge early — it is the highest-compliance-risk component and needs the most testing.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/modules/regional-cell/ — IaC module defining a complete regional cell (gateway, services, DB, event bus, metrics bridge)","infra/environments/{eu,us,apac}.tfvars — region-specific parameters (cloud region, compliance config, retention policies)","services/delivery-orchestrator/ — core workflow service, region-scoped","services/customer-service/ — customer PII management, region-scoped","services/courier-service/ — courier management with region-binding invariant","services/metrics-bridge/ — PII-stripping ACL component, per-region","services/global-metrics-aggregator/ — cross-region read model for dashboard","services/global-dashboard/ — real-time operations UI consuming aggregator","libs/domain-events/schemas/ — shared event schema definitions with PII field classification","libs/compliance/residency-validator/ — reusable module for domicile-region validation at gateway and service layers","docs/adr/001-multi-region-cell-architecture.md","docs/adr/002-no-cross-region-pii-replication.md","docs/adr/003-async-only-cross-region-communication.md","docs/runbooks/region-failover.md","docs/runbooks/customer-region-migration.md"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture for regional isolation. CQRS for separating regional commands from global reads. Anti-Corruption Layer (Metrics Bridge) for compliance boundary. Event-driven async for cross-region communication. Defense-in-depth for residency enforcement (GeoDNS + Gateway validation + service-level checks). Infrastructure-as-Code with parameterized regional deployments. Schema registry with PII classification for domain events.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["DO NOT create any database replication across regions — this is the most common way to accidentally violate data residency","DO NOT add synchronous cross-region calls to any customer-facing path — this will blow the 100ms latency budget and create availability coupling","DO NOT use a denylist approach for PII filtering in the Metrics Bridge — use an explicit allowlist of non-PII fields","DO NOT route customers by physical IP geolocation alone — use domicile region from the customer profile/auth token as the authoritative routing signal","DO NOT share a single event bus across regions — each region must have its own bus with the Metrics Bridge as the sole cross-region conduit","DO NOT allow courier region to be mutable without an explicit business process — a simple UPDATE would silently break the assignment constraint","DO NOT deploy a global distributed database (Spanner/CockroachDB) as the primary store — it creates implicit cross-region replication paths that undermine residency guarantees"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Data residency enforcement through cell isolation — this is a well-established pattern","Async-only cross-region communication for latency and isolation","Metrics Bridge as Anti-Corruption Layer for compliance","Cell-based architecture for blast radius containment","CQRS for separating regional writes from global reads"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact latency budget breakdown within the 100ms target depends on cloud provider region placement and database choice — needs benchmarking","Degraded service UX during regional partial outage needs product input on which features to prioritize in degraded mode","Global dashboard real-time expectations need quantification — 'real-time' could mean 1s or 30s, which affects Metrics Bridge batching strategy"],"assumptions":["Each customer has a single domicile region that determines data residency","Couriers operate in exactly one region and do not need cross-region mobility","The Global Operations Dashboard is used by internal operations staff, not customers","Current EU deployment can be refactored into the cell architecture without a full rewrite","Cloud provider supports the three target regions with equivalent service availability"]}}

trace.result: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics platform expanding from single-region EU to EU + US + APAC with strict data residency (GDPR/CCPA), region-bound courier assignment, a global operations dashboard, graceful degradation, and sub-100ms regional latency. The architecture uses a region-primary ownership model: each region owns its customer and courier data authoritatively, enforces residency at the infrastructure boundary, and publishes anonymized/aggregated operational metrics to a global read-only plane for the dashboard. Cross-region coordination is event-driven and asynchronous, with no synchronous cross-region calls on the customer hot path.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Regional Customer Service","responsibility":"Owns customer identity, PII, order history, and preferences for customers domiciled in that region. Enforces data residency — no replication of PII outside the region.","boundaries":"Deployed per-region. Database is region-local. No cross-region read/write. Exposes APIs only within its region's network boundary."},{"name":"Regional Courier Service","responsibility":"Manages courier profiles, availability, location tracking, and assignment eligibility. A courier is registered to exactly one region and can only be assigned deliveries within that region.","boundaries":"Deployed per-region. Courier records never replicate cross-region. Assignment logic enforces region constraint as an invariant."},{"name":"Regional Delivery Orchestrator","responsibility":"Coordinates order intake, courier assignment, route optimization, and delivery lifecycle within a single region. This is the core workflow engine.","boundaries":"Per-region deployment. Calls Regional Customer Service and Regional Courier Service within the same region only. Publishes domain events (delivery.created, delivery.completed, delivery.failed) to the regional event bus."},{"name":"Regional Event Bus","responsibility":"Intra-region event backbone for domain events. Fan-out to regional consumers and to the Global Metrics Aggregator via a sanitized bridge.","boundaries":"Per-region. Events containing PII stay on this bus. Only the Metrics Bridge component reads from it and forwards anonymized/aggregated data."},{"name":"Metrics Bridge (per-region)","responsibility":"Reads domain events from the regional event bus, strips all PII, computes regional aggregates (delivery counts, SLA metrics, courier utilization), and publishes sanitized metric events to the Global Metrics Topic.","boundaries":"Runs within the region. Strictly one-way: region → global. Never sends PII. Acts as the Anti-Corruption Layer between regional data and the global plane."},{"name":"Global Metrics Aggregator","responsibility":"Consumes sanitized metric events from all regions. Materializes a unified read model for the Global Operations Dashboard. Stores only non-PII aggregate data.","boundaries":"Deployed in a single coordination region (or multi-region active-active for HA). Contains zero PII. Read-only from the perspective of regional services."},{"name":"Global Operations Dashboard","responsibility":"Real-time dashboard showing cross-region KPIs: deliveries in progress, SLA compliance, courier utilization, regional health status.","boundaries":"Reads exclusively from the Global Metrics Aggregator. No direct access to any regional database or service."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Entry point for all customer and courier traffic in a region. Enforces authentication, rate limiting, and routes requests to regional services. GeoDNS directs users to their home region's gateway.","boundaries":"Per-region. Terminates TLS, enforces that requests are served by the correct region for the requesting user's domicile."},{"name":"Regional Resilience Layer","responsibility":"Circuit breakers, local caches, and fallback logic to provide degraded service when upstream dependencies (e.g., a regional DB replica) become unavailable. Includes a static/cached read path for critical customer-facing pages.","boundaries":"Per-region. Activates during partial failures to serve cached order status, estimated delivery times, and basic courier tracking."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Regional Delivery Orchestrator","contract":"REST/gRPC — CreateDelivery, GetDeliveryStatus, CancelDelivery. All requests include region-scoped customer ID.","coupling_type":"synchronous, region-local"},{"from":"Regional Delivery Orchestrator","to":"Regional Customer Service","contract":"gRPC — GetCustomer, ValidateCustomerRegion. Region-local only.","coupling_type":"synchronous, region-local"},{"from":"Regional Delivery Orchestrator","to":"Regional Courier Service","contract":"gRPC — FindAvailableCouriers, AssignCourier, ReleaseCourier. Region-scoped queries only.","coupling_type":"synchronous, region-local"},{"from":"Regional Delivery Orchestrator","to":"Regional Event Bus","contract":"Async publish — DeliveryCreated, DeliveryAssigned, DeliveryCompleted, DeliveryFailed events.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, fire-and-forget"},{"from":"Regional Event Bus","to":"Metrics Bridge","contract":"Async subscribe — consumes all delivery and courier domain events within the region.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, region-local consumer"},{"from":"Metrics Bridge","to":"Global Metrics Topic","contract":"Async publish — sanitized metric events (no PII): RegionDeliveryMetric, RegionCourierUtilization, RegionSLASnapshot.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, cross-region, one-way"},{"from":"Global Metrics Aggregator","to":"Global Metrics Topic","contract":"Async subscribe — consumes sanitized metrics from all regions.","coupling_type":"asynchronous, multi-region consumer"},{"from":"Global Operations Dashboard","to":"Global Metrics Aggregator","contract":"WebSocket/SSE for real-time push of aggregated metrics; REST for historical queries.","coupling_type":"synchronous read, no PII"}],"data_flow":"1) GeoDNS routes customer to their home Regional API Gateway based on domicile, not physical location. 2) Gateway forwards to Regional Delivery Orchestrator which coordinates with Regional Customer Service and Regional Courier Service — all within region. 3) Orchestrator publishes domain events to Regional Event Bus. 4) Metrics Bridge consumes events, strips PII, computes aggregates, publishes to Global Metrics Topic. 5) Global Metrics Aggregator materializes a unified view. 6) Dashboard reads from Aggregator via WebSocket. PII never crosses a regional boundary at any point in this flow.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Data Sovereignty by Design (Region-Primary Ownership)","rationale":"Each region is the single authoritative owner of its customer and courier data. No cross-region replication of PII. This satisfies GDPR data residency and CCPA compliance structurally — not by policy, but by architecture. There is no path for PII to leave the region."},{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (Metrics Bridge)","rationale":"The Metrics Bridge acts as an ACL between the rich, PII-laden regional domain model and the global metrics plane. It translates domain events into anonymized aggregates, preventing model leakage and ensuring compliance."},{"pattern":"CQRS (Command-Query Responsibility Segregation)","rationale":"Regional services handle commands (create delivery, assign courier). The Global Metrics Aggregator is a purely read-side projection optimized for dashboard queries. This separation allows independent scaling and avoids the global plane needing write access to regional data."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture with Async Bridging","rationale":"Cross-region communication is strictly asynchronous and one-way (region → global). No synchronous cross-region calls exist on the customer hot path, eliminating cross-region latency from the P99 budget and removing cross-region availability coupling."},{"pattern":"Cell-Based Architecture","rationale":"Each region is an independent cell with its own complete stack (gateway, services, database, event bus). Cells are isolated — a failure in the EU cell does not propagate to US or APAC. This directly supports the degraded-service-on-failure requirement."},{"pattern":"Bulkhead Isolation","rationale":"Regional isolation acts as a bulkhead. Additionally, within each region, the Resilience Layer provides circuit breakers and cached fallbacks to deliver degraded service during partial outages."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Delivery Management (per-region)","classification":"core","aggregates":["Delivery (root: DeliveryId, contains pickup/dropoff locations, status, assigned courier, customer reference — all region-scoped)","DeliveryAssignment (value object capturing courier-to-delivery binding with region constraint invariant)"]},{"name":"Customer Management (per-region)","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Customer (root: CustomerId, contains PII, domicile region — region is immutable after creation, enforces residency)"]},{"name":"Courier Management (per-region)","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Courier (root: CourierId, contains profile, home region, availability status, current location — region is immutable, enforces assignment constraint)"]},{"name":"Global Operations Intelligence","classification":"generic","aggregates":["RegionalMetricsSnapshot (root: RegionId + TimeBucket, contains delivery counts, SLA percentages, courier utilization — no PII)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Delivery Management","downstream":"Global Operations Intelligence","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer — Metrics Bridge strips PII from delivery events and publishes anonymized aggregates. Global context conforms to a sanitized published language."},{"upstream":"Customer Management","downstream":"Delivery Management","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Delivery Management queries Customer Management for validation but does not own customer data. Customer context dictates the model."},{"upstream":"Courier Management","downstream":"Delivery Management","relationship":"Customer-Supplier — Delivery Management queries Courier Management for availability and assignment. Courier context owns courier identity and region binding."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"No cross-region data replication for PII","gains":"Structural GDPR/CCPA compliance — impossible to violate residency by misconfiguration. Simpler audit posture. No cross-region replication lag for customer data.","sacrifices":"A customer who relocates from EU to US requires a formal data migration process (not a simple DB sync). Global customer search is not possible without a separate anonymized index.","alternatives_considered":["Encrypted cross-region replication with key management per region — rejected because it adds compliance surface area and key management complexity while not being structurally compliant","Single global database with row-level region tagging — rejected because a single misconfigured query or replication change could violate residency"]},{"decision":"Asynchronous-only cross-region communication","gains":"Eliminates cross-region latency from customer-facing paths (supports <100ms). Eliminates cross-region availability coupling (EU outage doesn't affect US). Simple failure model.","sacrifices":"Global dashboard shows near-real-time data (seconds of delay, not true real-time). Global consistency is eventual. Cannot perform synchronous cross-region transactions.","alternatives_considered":["Synchronous cross-region API calls for dashboard — rejected because it adds 100-300ms latency, creates availability coupling, and is unnecessary for a metrics dashboard","Global distributed database (e.g., CockroachDB/Spanner) — rejected because it introduces cross-region write latency and creates a path for PII to replicate"]},{"decision":"Cell-based architecture with independent stacks per region","gains":"Strong blast radius containment. Independent deployment and scaling per region. Regulatory independence — each region can have its own compliance configuration.","sacrifices":"Operational overhead of managing three independent deployments. Code duplication across regions (mitigated by shared IaC and CI/CD). Higher infrastructure cost than a single global deployment.","alternatives_considered":["Single global deployment with region routing at the application layer — rejected because it creates a single blast radius and makes residency enforcement application-level rather than infrastructure-level"]},{"decision":"Metrics Bridge as explicit component rather than direct event forwarding","gains":"Clear compliance boundary. Testable independently. Can be audited to prove no PII crosses regions. Allows metric schema evolution independent of domain events.","sacrifices":"Additional component to build, deploy, and monitor per region. Slight increase in event processing latency for metrics.","alternatives_considered":["Direct event forwarding with PII field filtering — rejected because implicit filtering is fragile; a new PII field added to a domain event could leak if the filter isn't updated"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., LATAM, MEA)","evolution_path":"The cell-based architecture means adding a region is deploying a new cell using the same IaC templates. The Metrics Bridge pattern means the Global Aggregator automatically picks up events from the new region's topic. No changes to existing regions required."},{"area":"Region-specific compliance rules","evolution_path":"Each region's services can have region-specific configuration (data retention periods, consent models, audit logging levels) without affecting other regions. The cell boundary is the compliance boundary."},{"area":"Dashboard evolution","evolution_path":"The Global Metrics Aggregator is a separate bounded context with its own read model. New dashboard views, alerting rules, or ML-based anomaly detection can be added without touching regional services."},{"area":"Cross-region customer migration","evolution_path":"A dedicated migration workflow can be built that exports customer data from the source region, transmits it securely, and imports it into the destination region with proper consent. This is an explicit, auditable process rather than implicit replication."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Customer domicile region binding","reason":"A customer's home region is immutable in normal operation. This is intentional — it enforces data residency structurally. Changing it requires an explicit migration workflow, not a simple update."},{"area":"Courier region binding","reason":"A courier's operating region is immutable. This enforces the business rule that a EU courier cannot be assigned to a US delivery. Cross-region courier operations would require a new business process, not a configuration change."},{"area":"No synchronous cross-region calls on the hot path","reason":"This is a load-bearing architectural constraint. Adding a synchronous cross-region call would violate the <100ms latency requirement and the regional isolation model. Any future feature requiring cross-region coordination must use async patterns."},{"area":"PII boundary at the Metrics Bridge","reason":"The Metrics Bridge is the sole gate between regional PII and the global plane. This boundary must never be bypassed. New metric requirements must go through the Bridge, not around it."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Clock skew and event ordering across regions causes inconsistent global dashboard metrics","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Use logical timestamps (vector clocks or Lamport timestamps) on metric events. The Global Aggregator uses time-bucketed aggregation windows (e.g., 10-second tumbling windows) that tolerate ordering variance. Dashboard shows time-bucket granularity, not event-level precision."},{"risk":"Metrics Bridge accidentally forwards PII due to domain event schema evolution","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Metrics Bridge uses an explicit allowlist of fields to forward (not a denylist). Automated CI tests verify that the Bridge output schema contains zero PII-classified fields. Schema registry enforces that new domain event fields are tagged with a PII classification. Audit log on all Bridge output."},{"risk":"GeoDNS misroutes a customer to the wrong region, causing latency or compliance issues","severity":"high","mitigation":"GeoDNS is the first-hop routing only. The Regional API Gateway performs a secondary check: it validates the customer's domicile region (from the auth token) against the current region. If mismatched, it returns a redirect to the correct region's gateway. This is a defense-in-depth measure."},{"risk":"EU region failure causes total loss of service for EU customers instead of graceful degradation","severity":"high","mitigation":"Within the EU cell, deploy across multiple availability zones. The Resilience Layer maintains a read-through cache of recent order statuses, courier locations, and delivery ETAs. During a partial outage (e.g., database failover), customers see cached data with a staleness indicator. New orders queue locally and process when the database recovers. Full-region failure (all AZs) is mitigated by multi-AZ redundancy — a simultaneous loss of all AZs is an accepted residual risk."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of managing three independent cells leads to configuration drift","severity":"medium","mitigation":"All three regions are deployed from the same Infrastructure-as-Code templates with region-specific parameter files. CI/CD pipeline deploys to all regions with canary rollouts. Configuration drift detection runs daily and alerts on divergence."},{"risk":"Global Metrics Aggregator becomes a single point of failure for the dashboard","severity":"low","mitigation":"The Aggregator is a read-only projection that can be rebuilt from the Global Metrics Topic. Deploy it in a multi-AZ configuration in the coordination region. If it fails, the dashboard shows stale data; no regional operations are affected."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout in three stages. Phase 1: Refactor the existing EU monolith into the cell architecture (Regional Gateway, Delivery Orchestrator, Customer Service, Courier Service, Event Bus) while remaining single-region. Validate the architecture works. Phase 2: Deploy the US cell using the same IaC templates, implement the Metrics Bridge and Global Aggregator, bring up the Global Dashboard. Phase 3: Deploy the APAC cell. Each phase is independently valuable and reduces risk. Start with the Metrics Bridge early — it is the highest-compliance-risk component and needs the most testing.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/modules/regional-cell/ — IaC module defining a complete regional cell (gateway, services, DB, event bus, metrics bridge)","infra/environments/{eu,us,apac}.tfvars — region-specific parameters (cloud region, compliance config, retention policies)","services/delivery-orchestrator/ — core workflow service, region-scoped","services/customer-service/ — customer PII management, region-scoped","services/courier-service/ — courier management with region-binding invariant","services/metrics-bridge/ — PII-stripping ACL component, per-region","services/global-metrics-aggregator/ — cross-region read model for dashboard","services/global-dashboard/ — real-time operations UI consuming aggregator","libs/domain-events/schemas/ — shared event schema definitions with PII field classification","libs/compliance/residency-validator/ — reusable module for domicile-region validation at gateway and service layers","docs/adr/001-multi-region-cell-architecture.md","docs/adr/002-no-cross-region-pii-replication.md","docs/adr/003-async-only-cross-region-communication.md","docs/runbooks/region-failover.md","docs/runbooks/customer-region-migration.md"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture for regional isolation. Defense-in-depth for residency enforcement (GeoDNS + Gateway validation + service-level checks). Infrastructure-as-Code with parameterized regional deployments. Schema registry with PII classification for domain events.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["DO NOT create any database replication across regions — this is the most common way to accidentally violate data
